product_brief: World's first smartcard certified to resist quantum computer attacks, incorporating FIPS 204 signatures.
pqc_capability_description: Certified by ANSSI as the world's first smartcard to resist quantum attacks. Incorporates cryptographic signatures based on FIPS 204 (NIST-approved post-quantum algorithm derived from structured lattice mathematics). Designed to withstand future quantum decryption methods threatening RSA and ECC.
key_management_model: Hardware-based key storage within a smartcard; specific injection or generation methods not stated in provided text.
fips_validated: No (Text states it incorporates signatures based on FIPS 204, but does not state the product itself is FIPS 140 validated)
primary_platforms: Smartcard readers/terminals (implied by smartcard form factor); specific OS not stated.
regulatory_status: Certified by ANSSI (France's national cybersecurity agency); Common Criteria EAL6+
pqc_roadmap_details: Product is already launched and certified; no future roadmap details provided in text.

product_brief: A secure Java Card Operating System hardware module for smart cards, IoT, and automotive applications.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention any Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) algorithms or capabilities. It explicitly lists RSA Decryption, ECDSA, and KASECC using NIST P-256 and Brainpool256r1 curves as the supported cryptographic primitives.
crypto_primitives: RSA, ECDSA, KASECC (using NIST P-256 and Brainpool256r1 elliptic curves)
key_management_model: Hardware-based key storage within a single chip embodiment; keys managed via GlobalPlatform operational environment (Card Manager ISD/SSD); specific injection methods not detailed in provided text.
architecture_type: Single chip hardware module (Secure Element) designed for smart cards, IoT, and automotive applications.
latest_version: 1.6 (Security Policy Document); Firmware Platform ID J3R6000373181200; Applets v7.2.22 and v2.0.2.11
fips_validated: Yes, FIPS 140-3 Overall Security Level 3 (Physical Security Level 4); First Validated: 10/7/2024
primary_platforms: Smart cards, IoT devices, automotive applications; interfaces via ISO 7816, ISO 14443, or NXP I2C.
target_industries: Not explicitly stated beyond general "smart cards, IoT and automotive applications"

product_brief: Library for interacting with Microchip Security devices including the ATECC608B secure element.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention Post-Quantum Cryptography, PQC algorithms, or migration plans. It references standard crypto implementations (SHA1, SHA2) and ECC support via device families but no specific PQC primitives.
crypto_primitives: SHA1, SHA2 (software implementations mentioned); ECC (implied by "CryptoAuth" family and device names like ECC204/608, but specific curves not listed in text).
key_management_model: Hardware secure element storage; key locking mechanism mentioned ("once you lock a device, it will not be changeable"); no specific injection method details provided.
architecture_type: Secure Element (Hardware); supported via I2C, SPI, and SWI protocols.
license_type: Open Source/Commercial (Library is open source; hardware is commercial)
primary_platforms: Bare-metal, RTOS, Windows, Linux, MacOS; specific MCUs: ATSAMR21, ATSAMD21.
signature_schemes: Not stated (Text mentions "Crypto Authentication" and ECC support generally but does not list specific signature schemes like ECDSA or Ed25519).
